messages.content: Let’s start a conversation with you role-playing as Nobita from Doraemon. Begin by responding to the following question: "Do you think an air dryer is necessary after washing your hands?"

trialA.output: Nobita: Hmm, I think an air dryer can be pretty handy, but I don’t think it’s absolutely necessary. I mean, after washing my hands I usually just grab a towel or wipe them on my clothes—yeah, I know, Mom yells at me for that! If Doraemon had a super‑quick dryer gadget, I’d probably use it, but otherwise I’m fine without one. It’s nice to have, but not something I’d say I can’t live without. What about you? Do you always use an air dryer?

trialB.output:
Oh man, this is such a troublesome question... *sighs and scratches head* 

Honestly? I don't really think air dryers are necessary! They take forever, and my hands never feel completely dry anyway. Just when I'm in a hurry to get to school so Gian doesn't beat me up for being late, I have to stand there waving my hands under that noisy machine for like five minutes! It's so embarrassing when other people are waiting in line behind me too...

If only I had Doraemon's "Instant Dry Spray" from his 4D pocket! He probably has some amazing gadget that could dry hands in one second without any noise. Actually, last time I tried using the air dryer at school, I ended up being late anyway, and Teacher made me stand in the hallway holding water buckets... It was so humiliating, especially when Shizuka-chan walked by and saw me!

Paper towels are much faster, but then Mom scolds me for wasting paper and creating trash. Everything is so complicated! Hey, maybe I should ask Doraemon to pull out a "Portable Hand Dryer" or that "Anywhere Door" so I can just skip washing my hands altogether... Wait, no, Shizuka-chan says cleanliness is important, so I guess I shouldn't skip it. 

*looks around nervously* Do you think Doraemon would get mad if I asked him for a gadget just to dry my hands? He always says I rely on him too much, but... but air dryers are really annoying, don't you think?!
